summary: Video set to music with no speech, with a yellow circular 'U.S. BORDER PATROL' logo throughout. It moves from a black-and-white archival photo of two early agents beside a stack of liquor barrels and bottles (caption 'THE U.S. BORDER PATROL') to footage of agents in several settings: two on snowmobiles on snow, a gray patrol boat on water at dawn, two agents carrying a person on a litter down a desert road, and two agents on horseback in cowboy hats. The right rider on horseback resembles Secretary Kristi Noem.

script.scene: Yellow circular 'U.S. BORDER PATROL' logo top-left over a framed black-and-white photo of two early agents standing beside a large stack of liquor barrels and bottles; caption 'THE U.S. BORDER PATROL'.
Two agents in green cold-weather uniforms and helmets ride snowmobiles across a white snowfield; logo persists.
A gray Border Patrol rigid-hull patrol boat (hull number 274303) moves across calm water at dawn.
Two agents in helmets and packs carry a person strapped to a litter down a dusty desert road, another figure ahead in the haze.
Two agents on horseback in cowboy hats and green uniforms ride through bare-tree brush; the right rider resembles Secretary Kristi Noem.

notable_text: tweet: On the 101st anniversary of the U.S. Border Patrol, we thank all who've worn the green for their honorable service. We will ALWAYS have your back!
